name: Bitquark
why_notable: Published bitquark-subdomains-top100000.txt — a 100,000-entry subdomain wordlist compiled from web crawl data, making it structurally different from the CT log + passive DNS methodology behind Jason Haddix's top1million series. The list was picked up by SecLists and became a standard complement because the two datasets have different blind spots: crawl data catches subdomains that were never formally registered in CT logs; passive DNS catches ones that had live DNS records. Nobody knows who bitquark actually is — no blog, no conference presence, no linked profiles. The methodology matters more than the identity.
